event: 69-year-old woman
stabbed herself in the abdomen with a kitchen knife
suicidal intent
admitted to our emergency department
blood pressure could not be measured
cardiac monitor showed pulseless electrical activity
body temperature was 35.0 °C
oxygen administered via 10-L oxygen reservoir mask
oxygen saturation was 99%
Glasgow Coma Scale score of 3 (E1V1M1)
developed agonal respiration
wound measuring 5 cm present on upper abdomen
ultrasonography revealed intra-abdominal fluid collection
emergency thoracotomy was performed
aortic cross-clamping was performed
open cardiac massage was performed
aorta was clamped for 15 min
epinephrine 1 mg administered thrice at 3-min intervals
temporary return of spontaneous circulation was observed
still hemodynamically unstable
laparotomy was performed
injuries to the common hepatic artery
injuries to the splenic artery
injuries to the pancreas
injuries to the spleen
injuries to the liver
injured arteries were ligated to achieve hemostasis
left gastric artery was not identified
branch arising directly from the aorta was injured
ligation was performed on the branch from the aorta
distal pancreatectomy was performed
splenectomy was performed
liver was sutured
norepinephrine was administered at 0.25 μg/kg/min intraoperatively
second-look surgery was performed
no signs of active bleeding
no signs of ischemic change macroscopically
vasopressor requirement was no longer noted
abdominal wall closure was performed
enhanced computed tomography scan was performed
computed tomography scan demonstrated disruption of the celiac artery
gastroduodenal artery was seen arising from superior mesenteric artery as collateral flow
right gastric arteries were not observed during CT angiography
left gastric artery was not observed during CT angiography
right gastroepiploic artery was not observed during CT angiography
left gastroepiploic artery was not observed during CT angiography
short gastric arteries were not observed during CT angiography
gastroscopy was performed
gastroscopy revealed patchy mucosal necrosis on the gastric upper body
conservative treatment was administered
patient developed fever of 39 °C
complained of pain in the stomach
blood examination revealed white blood cell count of 34,000/mm³
blood examination revealed C-reactive protein of 13.4 mg/dL
computed tomography scan was performed
computed tomography scan showed presence of air in gastric wall
computed tomography scan showed intra-abdominal free air
gastric necrosis was suspected
gastroscopy was performed
gastroscopy revealed extensive mucosal necrosis of the stomach
emergency surgery was performed
necrosis of the stomach was revealed
total gastrectomy with Roux-en-Y reconstruction was performed
histological findings of the stomach revealed diffuse necrotic changes
histological findings revealed various inflammatory cell infiltrations including numerous neutrophils and eosinophils
no evidence of invasive fungal infection was noted
leakage on duodenal stump identified
continuous tube drainage was performed
consciousness became clear
underwent rehabilitation in bed for a few weeks
developed sepsis due to multidrug-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa infection
developed disseminated intravascular coagulation
patient's general condition gradually deteriorated
patient died 70 days after admission
